summary: The agent evaluates whether a temporary resin bridge restores occlusion to the same extent as a permanent one. Although there’s no direct textual support, clinically reasonable practice assumes such bridgess maintain adequate occlusal contact to stabilize adjacent teeth during treatment planning.
